answer:
The Model Context Protocol (MCP) allows AI assistants to interact directly with Neo4j graph databases [1][2]. There are two primary sources for these servers: the official Neo4j MCP server and the Neo4j Labs suite [1][3].

The official server focuses on core database operations like executing read/write Cypher queries, retrieving graph schemas, and listing GDS procedures [1][2]. The Neo4j Labs collection provides specialized servers for tasks like knowledge graph memory management, Aura cloud administration, and interactive data modeling [3][4][5].

You can install the official server via PyPI [1]:
```bash
pip install neo4j-mcp-server
```

Configuration involves defining the server in your MCP client's configuration file (e.g., mcp.json) with your database credentials and connection URI [1][6]. These servers support multiple transport modes, including STDIO for local tools like Claude Desktop and HTTP/SSE for remote or scalable deployments [2][3].

results.title: Model Context Protocol (MCP) Integrations for the Neo4j Graph Database - Developer Guides
Getting started with MCP servers: A technical deep dive
